source.definition: An exclamation expressing surprise, astonishment, or mild disbelief, similar to saying 'Well, I never!' or 'You don't say!'.

explanation.sourceText: The phrase "Well, I'll be!" is a fixed English expression combining the interjection "Well" (used to express surprise or to draw attention) and the phrase "I'll be" which implies astonishment or disbelief.
explanation.targetText: Expresia engleză „Well, I'll be!” este o construcție fixă formată din exclamația „Well” (folosită pentru a exprima surpriză sau a atrage atenția) și expresia „I'll be” care exprimă uimire sau neîncredere.

target.definition: O exclamație folosită pentru a exprima surpriză, uimire sau ușoară neîncredere, echivalentă cu „Na sowas!” în germană.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian phrase "Ce chestie!" consists of the interrogative pronoun "ce" (what) and the noun "chestie" (thing, matter), which is borrowed from Slavic.
explanation.targetText: Expresia românească „Ce chestie!” este formată din pronumele interogativ „ce” (ce) și substantivul „chestie” (lucru, chestie), care provine dintr-un împrumut slav.

etymology.source: Dicționar etimologic român, Studien zur Sprachgeschichte

examples.sourceText: Well, I'll be! I didn't expect that.
examples.targetText: Ce chestie! Nu m-aș fi așteptat la asta.

examples.sourceText: Well, I'll be, you're already finished!
Well, I'll be, I've never seen that before.
examples.targetText: Ce chestie, nu am mai văzut așa ceva până acum.

learningTips.text: Imagine you discover something totally unexpected and say, "Well, I'll be!" In Romanian, you can think of someone surprised pointing at something and saying "Ce chestie!", meaning "What a thing!".
The word "chestie" sounds a bit like the English word "chesty" (relating to the chest), but here it means "thing" or "matter"—something you can point out or discover.

"Well" in English is a short, often surprised interjection. In Romanian, you replace it with "Ce" (what), which is also used in questions—this helps you remember the surprise as a question about "What?"

Connect "Well, I'll be!" with a little story: You find something on the ground and say "Well, I'll be!" In Romanian, you say "Ce chestie!", as if saying "What a thing!"—this helps keep the phrase vivid in your mind.
